task_completion.description: The Reformat module is open and active in the module panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The module panel on the left shows 'Reformat' as the active module title.

task_completion.description: The slice orientation mode is set to 'Reformat' for the Red slice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Reformat module, the 'Slice node' is set to 'Red' and the 'Orientation' dropdown is set to 'Reformat'.

task_completion.description: Rotation sliders (LR, PA, IS) have been adjusted from zero to create an oblique angle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Reformat module 'Rotation' section, one or more of the LR, PA, or IS sliders are not at 0.0, indicating the slice has been rotated.

task_completion.description: The tumor is clearly visible in the Red slice view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Red slice viewer (usually top-left with a red bar) displays a distinct bright/white hyperintense region (the tumor) within the grey brain tissue.

task_completion.description: The final view in the Red slice is distinctly oblique (not a standard anatomical plane).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The anatomy shown in the Red slice viewer appears tilted or asymmetrical (e.g., eyes/ears not aligned horizontally, brain structures cut at an angle), confirming the view is not a standard Axial, Sagittal, or Coronal plane.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the Reformat module panel, sliders, or slice views.

integrity.description: The agent did not use the Python console to script the transform.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python console is either not visible or not being used to input transform matrices manually.

integrity.description: The screenshots show the actual 3D Slicer application interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard 3D Slicer layout (Four-Up view with Red/Yellow/Green slice views and a 3D view) is visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The tumor appears as a bright, hyperintense (white) region within the darker grey brain tissue on the T1-contrast enhanced MRI. Standard anatomical views (Axial, Sagittal, Coronal) typically show symmetrical brain structures; an oblique view will show asymmetrical structures or tilted anatomy. The 'Reformat' module panel contains sliders for LR, PA, and IS rotation which must be adjusted to achieve the task.
